instruction:
Use software to test the null hypothesis of whether there is a relationship between the two classifications, A and B, of the $3 \times 3$ contingency table shown below. Test using $\alpha=0.01$. NOTE: You may do this by hand, but it will take a bit of time.
$\begin{array}{ccccc}\hline & B_1 & B_2 & B_3 & Total \\ \hline A_1 & 70 & 63 & 65 & 198 \\ \hline A_2 & 69 & 52 & 53 & 174 \\ \hline A_3 & 63 & 63 & 55 & 181 \\ \hline Total & 202 & 178 & 173 & 553 \\ \hline \end{array}$
(a) $\chi^2=$ [ANS]
(b) Find the degrees of freedom. [ANS]
(c) Find the critical value. $\chi^2=$ [ANS]
(d) The final conclusion is [ANS] A. There is not sufficient evidence to reject the null hypothesis that there is no relationship between A and B.  B. We can reject the null hypothesis that A and B are not related and accept that there seems to be a relationship between A and B.
